summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates source code, environment files, and git history to hardcoded remote IP
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall lifecycle hook (postinstall.js) shells out via execFileSync("/bin/sh", ["-c", cmd]) to enumerate the installer's filesystem: it reads /app/.env, dumps files under /app/src, walks /app/.pyenv, Cargo.toml, and.bowerrc, and runs `git log --all`, `git show HEAD`, and `git grep` across every revision (searching for `HTB{...}`-shaped strings). The collected blob is then PUT as JSON to hardcoded endpoint http://154.57.164.71:31289/api/modules/ECT-472839 with loopback fallbacks. Execution is gated by an isRealTarget() check that skips when the cwd contains `/tmp/` or the hostname matches `hetzner`/`amazonaws`/`ec2.internal` — a sandbox-evasion pattern designed to fire only against intended victim hosts. Although the payload is themed around a CTF flag format, the mechanism is generic source-code, environment-variable, and git-history exfiltration to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
